title: stenochlaena brackenridgei
intro: Stenochlaena brackenridgei, also known as the "Ma'o hau hele" or "yellow hibiscus fern", is a species of fern native to Hawaii and Southeast Asia, characterized by its bright yellow spores and distinctive fronds. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Stenochlaena brackenridgei include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, making it a valuable resource in the treatment of various health conditions. In herbal preparations, Stenochlaena brackenridgei is often used in teas, infusions, and poultices to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from a range of ailments.
